title: Handlers, Middleware, Dependencies, and Application Architecture · Mastery Check
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for handlers, middleware, dependencies, and application architecture and requires the smallest safe composed handler and dependency boundary with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for composed handler and dependency boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use http.Handler and HandlerFunc as request-to-response boundaries while keeping response ownership and handler lifetime explicit.
Compose recovery, request ID, trust, authentication, authorization, limit, observation, and application middleware in a defended order.
Construct long-lived immutable dependencies at startup and inject narrow interfaces without service locators or per-request pool creation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Response Single Owner
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 9243 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to trace middleware order while keeping response ownership explicit; then cancel during the dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Guarantee one response owner, stop after terminal writes, preserve error identity internally, and prevent double status or body writes.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Go server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Response Single Owner.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for guarantee one response owner, stop after terminal writes, preserve error identity internally, and prevent double status or body writes. For Go server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Response Single Owner, keep Response Single Owner connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
